name: Chipiona from El Puerto de Santa María – circular via Rota
name_original: Chipiona desde El Puerto de Santa María – circular por Rota
region_intro: Road cycling around El Puerto De Santa María offers diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths along the Bay of Cádiz to routes through vineyards and areas with gentle rolling hills. The region is characterized by its extensive coastline, salt marshes, and the Guadalete River, providing varied backdrops for cycling. Well-paved surfaces are common, with some routes extending into the wider Cádiz province. This area serves as a starting point for numerous road cycling routes, including circular options around the Bahía de Cádiz Natural Park. Best road cycling routes around El Puerto De Santa María The most popular road cycling route is Bahía de Cádiz – circular from El Puerto de Santa María, a 47.5 miles (76.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of over 400 meters. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Chipiona from El Puerto de Santa María – circular via Rota, a moderate 44.0 miles (70.9 km) path. This route offers coastal views and passes through the town of Rota. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo de San Marcos – Castle of Doña Blanca loop from Las Redes, a 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail leading through areas with gentle hills, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around El Puerto De Santa María is defined by coastal paths, salt marshes, and vineyard landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in El Puerto De Santa María are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Puerto De Santa María's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in El Puerto de Santa María?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available in El Puerto de Santa María, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in El Puerto de Santa María?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths along the Bay of Cádiz to routes through vineyards and areas with gentle rolling hills. While well-paved surfaces are common, some routes may extend into the wider Cádiz province, providing varied backdrops of salt marshes and the Guadalete River.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Puerto de Santa María?
A: El Puerto de Santa María boasts excellent cycling weather year-round. The most comfortable temperatures for extended cycling seasons are typically from March through November, making it an ideal destination for most of the year.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in El Puerto de Santa María?
A: Yes, the area offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the Natural Park of the Bay of Cadiz features easy cycling routes with mostly paved surfaces. You can find 7 easy routes listed in the komoot guide for the region.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes in the region?
A: Yes, for more experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are routes with greater elevation gains and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31609375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chipiona Sign – Cádiz Ferry Crossing loop from El Puerto de Santa María&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult route covering over 111 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around El Puerto de Santa María?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977097194/bahia-de-cadiz-circular-from-el-puerto-de-santa-maria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bahía de Cádiz – circular from El Puerto de Santa María&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 76.4 km trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes often pass through the Bahía de Cádiz Natural Park, where you can observe lush vegetation, lagoons, and salt marshes, home to various bird species like flamingos. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3015347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cádiz Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2813433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ficus del Mora&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4902517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda de Apodaca Promenade&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views.

Q: Are there routes that pass through the famous Jerez Wine Country?
A: Yes, the surrounding agricultural landscapes include vineyards, offering scenic routes that can be incorporated into cycling tours. These routes provide a taste of the region's famous sherry production, allowing you to cycle through picturesque wine country.

Q: Which routes offer coastal views or access to beaches?
A: Many routes in El Puerto de Santa María feature stunning coastal views. The town boasts 22 kilometers of coastline with golden sandy beaches like Playa de Valdelagrana. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975015198/chipiona-from-el-puerto-de-santa-maria-circular-via-rota" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chipiona from El Puerto de Santa María – circular via Rota&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, offers beautiful coastal scenery.

Q: Is El Puerto de Santa María suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: Yes, areas like the Los Toruños and La Algaida Natural Park offer an 18-kilometer circular route ideal for family cycling, with direct access to the beach and opportunities to rent bicycles within the park. The mostly paved surfaces and gentle terrain make it suitable for all ages.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in El Puerto de Santa María?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, El Puerto de Santa María, as a popular tourist destination, offers various public parking options throughout the town and near popular attractions and beaches, which can serve as starting points for your rides.

Q: Are there opportunities to combine cycling with other activities, like visiting wineries?
A: Absolutely. El Puerto de Santa María is renowned for its sherry wine. Prominent bodegas like Osborne and Terry offer tours and tasting experiences, which can be easily incorporated into your cycling itinerary, especially on routes passing through the Jerez Wine Country.

Q: Can I take a ferry to Cádiz with my bike from El Puerto de Santa María?
A: Yes, a convenient and pleasant 30-minute ferry ride connects El Puerto de Santa María to the historic city of Cádiz. This offers an excellent option for a day trip, allowing you to explore Cádiz by bike after your ferry journey.

To see the landscape you have to climb 322 steps :) to visit the lighthouse it is essential to book the visit at the Chipiona tourist office (956-929065)

The singer's bronze monument is located in a roundabout and consists of an approximately 2.40 meter high statue of the image of Rocío Jurado, surrounded by approximately 8 meter high pilasters. It is a gift from the artist Juan de Ávalos.
